perturbation.original_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as confidential; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to those of its employees and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text of Clause 2 outlines specific exceptions to the confidentiality obligations, such as disclosure compelled by law or use for 'LOA purposes'. Removing these exception clauses creates an in-text contradiction. The Receiving Party is now obligated to treat all Confidential Information as strictly confidential without any exceptions. This omission directly contradicts Clause 6, which acknowledges that the NDA may involve the exchange of classified information governed by applicable laws and regulations. If disclosure is compelled by law, as allowed under Clause 6, it would contradict the absolute confidentiality now stipulated in Clause 2. Removing 'LOA purposes' also creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 6 The NDA may also involve the exchange of classified information (which may or may not be Confidential Information), the handling of which shall be treated by the Parties according to applicable laws and regulations and other administrative acts and policies, now or hereinafter in effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes exceptions to the confidentiality obligations, specifically 'except where compelled by law or regulation' and 'except for LOA purposes'. This creates a contradiction with Clause 6, which acknowledges the exchange of classified information governed by applicable laws and regulations. If disclosure is compelled by law, as allowed under Clause 6, it would contradict the absolute confidentiality now stipulated in Clause 2 of the changed text. This meets the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 5 Either Party shall, upon 30 days written request by the other Party, return to the other party all Confidential Information which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof together with a confirmation in writing that the Confidential Information in computerized form has been destroyed. Furthermore, at the request of the Disclosing Party the Receiving Party shall immediately cease to use the Confidential Information for any purposes whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 5 Either Party shall return to the other party all Confidential Information which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof. Furthermore, at the request of the Disclosing Party the Receiving Party shall cease to use the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original Clause 5 mandates a 30-day written notice for the return of confidential information and requires written confirmation of the destruction of computerized copies. Omitting the 30-day notice and the confirmation of destruction creates an in-text contradiction. Without the notice period, the clause implies immediate return upon request, but Clause 12 states that the obligations under the NDA, including Clause 5, extend for five years after termination. This means a party could demand immediate return years after the initial exchange, which is inconsistent with the expected use of the information over that period. Similarly, without confirmation of destruction of the digital data, it creates uncertainty about whether all the data has been permanently deleted, since at the request of the disclosing party, the Receiving party needs to cease the use of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 12 The NDA shall become effective on the date on the date of signatures and shall be valid for a period of two years thereafter, when it shall automatically expire 4 unless otherwise agreed in writing between the parties. However, all Confidential Information exchanged during the validity hereof shall be subject to the obligations and restrictions under the NDA (including the undertakings in Clause 13 and 14 below) for a period of five years following expiration or other termination of the NDA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 30-day written notice and the requirement for written confirmation of data destruction, which directly contradicts Clause 12. Clause 12 extends the obligations under the NDA, including those related to confidential information, for five years after termination. The removal implies an immediate return upon request, conflicting with the extended obligations and potentially disrupting the use of the confidential information as contemplated by the NDA for those five years. The absence of destruction confirmation creates uncertainty about whether all data has been permanently deleted when the Receiving Party needs to cease using the information.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 10 For the purpose of exchanging Confidential Information and other communication the following persons are authorized contact persons: For GI: Fredrik Gustafsson Campaign Manager, Gripen International SE-581 88 Linkoping Sweden For NMOD: Norwegian Defence Logistic Organisation New Fighter Aircraft Project (P7600) Att: Lt. col. Geir Nilssen P.box 10 2027 Kjeller Norway A change of a contact person shall be notified in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 10 For the purpose of exchanging Confidential Information and other communication the following persons are authorized contact persons: For GI: Fredrik Gustafsson Campaign Manager, Gripen International SE-581 88 Linkoping Sweden For NMOD: Norwegian Defence Logistic Organisation New Fighter Aircraft Project (P7600) Att: Lt. col. Geir Nilssen P.box 10 2027 Kjeller Norway
perturbation.explanation: The original Clause 10 specifies contact persons and states that 'A change of a contact person shall be notified in writing.' Removing this sentence creates an in-text contradiction. Clause 8 requires the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party immediately if Confidential Information is disclosed to an unauthorized person. Without a requirement to notify changes in contact persons, it becomes unclear who is authorized to receive information, potentially leading to unintentional breaches of confidentiality without triggering the notification obligation in Clause 8, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 8 The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party immediately if it becomes aware that Confidential Information has been disclosed to or is in the possession of any person who is not authorized by the NDA to receive Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original Clause 10 requires notification of contact person changes in writing. The changed version removes this requirement. Clause 8 requires immediate notification if confidential information is disclosed to an unauthorized person. Without the notification requirement in Clause 10, it is unclear who is authorized, potentially leading to unauthorized disclosures that are not reported, thus creating a contradiction between the changed Clause 10 and Clause 8. The omission directly conflicts with the obligation to report unauthorized disclosures, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 13 The NDA shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws and regulations of Norway.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 13 The NDA shall be governed by the laws and regulations of Norway.
perturbation.explanation: The original Clause 13 specifies that the NDA shall be 'governed by and construed in accordance with' Norwegian laws. Removing 'construed in accordance with' introduces ambiguity. While the NDA is still governed by Norwegian law, the omission suggests that the interpretation or construction of the agreement may not necessarily align with Norwegian legal principles. This contradicts Clause 14, which stipulates that disputes shall be settled in a Norwegian court of law. If the agreement is not consistently interpreted under Norwegian law, the court's ability to resolve disputes based on a shared understanding of the agreement is undermined, since by removing the 'construed in accordance with' and still setting in Clause 14: 'Any disputes between the Parties arising out of or in connection with the NDA and which cannot be settled amicably shall be settled in the common court of law'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 14 Any disputes between the Parties arising out of or in connection with the NDA and which cannot be settled amicably shall be settled in the common court of law. The legal venue is Oslo tingrett, Norway.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the NDA shall be 'governed by and construed in accordance with' Norwegian laws. The changed text omits 'construed in accordance with,' which means the interpretation of the agreement may not align with Norwegian legal principles. This contradicts Clause 14, which stipulates that disputes shall be settled in a Norwegian court of law, because the court's ability to resolve disputes depends on a shared understanding of the agreement under Norwegian law which is compromised by the omission. This follows the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 12 The NDA shall become effective on the date on the date of signatures and shall be valid for a period of two years thereafter, when it shall automatically expire 4 unless otherwise agreed in writing between the parties. However, all Confidential Information exchanged during the validity hereof shall be subject to the obligations and restrictions under the NDA (including the undertakings in Clause 13 and 14 below) for a period of five years following expiration or other termination of the NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 12 The NDA shall become effective on the date on the date of signatures and shall be valid for a period of two years thereafter, when it shall automatically expire unless otherwise agreed in writing between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original Clause 12 specifies that the NDA is valid for two years and then continues to apply to the Confidential Information for five years following its expiration. By removing the text 'However, all Confidential Information exchanged during the validity hereof shall be subject to the obligations and restrictions under the NDA (including the undertakings in Clause 13 and 14 below) for a period of five years following expiration or other termination of the NDA', the NDA is now only valid for the initial two years. This contradicts Clause 5, which states that upon request, the receiving party shall cease using the Confidential Information, implying an ongoing obligation. With this removed clause, if the other party request the data back and that the receving party ceases to use the Confidential Information, after the first two years the receiver can simply dispose of it, and there is nothing legally binding to avoid it, since the duration of the NDA its just for two years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Clause 5 Either Party shall return to the other party all Confidential Information which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof. Furthermore, at the request of the Disclosing Party the Receiving Party shall cease to use the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original Clause 12 extended the confidentiality obligations beyond the initial two-year term of the NDA. The changed Clause 12 removes this extension, limiting the NDA's validity to only two years. This directly contradicts Clause 5, which requires the receiving party to cease using confidential information 'at the request of the Disclosing Party,' implying an ongoing obligation that extends beyond the initial two-year period. By removing the extended confidentiality obligations, the changed clause creates a conflict with Clause 5, making it a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
